title: Taktouka
subtitle: Moroccan roasted green pepper and tomato salad
excerpt: Green peppers and tomatoes roasted until charred, then peeled and cooked down together with garlic, cumin, and olive oil into a warm salad. Served at room temperature as part of the Moroccan first course. The char on the peppers gives a mild smokiness; the tomatoes give sweetness; the cumin ties everything together.
sourceNotes: Taktouka is one of the cooked salad plates that precede the main course of a Moroccan meal, alongside zaalouk, carrot salad, and briouats. The combination of roasted green pepper and tomato is also found in Tunisian mechouia (with mixed peppers). The Moroccan version uses green pepper specifically.

content.text: The peppers must be properly charred for this recipe to have its characteristic flavour. Roast or grill them until the skins are blackened and blistered, then steam them in a covered bowl for 10 minutes, this loosens the skins completely. Peel under cold running water and discard all skin and seeds.

The tomatoes are cooked briefly in olive oil with garlic and cumin after the peppers are peeled and sliced. The whole thing comes together in under 10 minutes once the peppers are peeled. Serve warm or at room temperature; cold from the fridge it loses its appeal.

Roast the {{pepper-green}} directly over a gas flame or under a very hot grill, turning with tongs, until the skins are completely charred all over, about 10 minutes. Transfer to a bowl, cover tightly with cling film, and leave for 10 minutes. Peel, deseed, and cut the peppers into strips. Heat the {{olive-oil}} in a pan over medium heat. Add the {{garlic}}, {{cumin-ground}}, and {{paprika-sweet}} and cook for 30 seconds. Add the diced {{tomato}} and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Cook for 5 to 7 minutes until the tomatoes have softened and released their liquid. Add the pepper strips and stir to combine. Cook for 3 more minutes. Remove from the heat and stir in the {{lemon-juice}} and {{coriander}}. Serve warm or at room temperature.

items.fix: The skins must be completely black, not just a few patches. The 10-minute steam inside a covered bowl is the key step that loosens the skin
items.cause: Peppers not charred enough or not steamed after charring

items.fix: Use ripe, fleshier tomatoes. Increase the cooking time to drive off more moisture before adding the peppers
items.cause: Tomatoes too liquid or not cooked down enough

items.fix: Oven roasting produces soft peppers without the char. A gas flame or very hot grill is needed for the authentic result. Increase the oven to maximum with the grill on if no gas flame is available
items.cause: Peppers roasted in the oven rather than over a flame

content.text: Taktouka is one of the trio of Moroccan cooked salads that open the meal alongside zaalouk and carrot salad. In Moroccan homes these small plates are set on the table before the tagine or couscous arrives, for tearing bread and dipping. Taktouka is also made across North Africa with variations in pepper colour and seasoning. In Britain it is a useful make-ahead side dish for roast chicken or lamb, and it keeps well in the fridge.
